LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH MEDICAL FOUNDATION'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OF AMENDED COMPLAINT ON DIGNITY HEALTH MEDICAL FOUNDATION IS DENIED IN ITS ENTIRETY. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO PLAINTIFFS ABELL, DRIEMEYER, AND HALL AS MOVANT CONCEDES THAT IT HAS GENERALLY APPEARED IN THEIR ACTION AND NO LONGER SEEKS TO QUASH SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS. (REPLY, 2:1-6.) THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO PLAINTIFF NAVA. "CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 474 IS TO BE LIBERALLY CONSTRUED." (FULLER V. TUCKER (2000) 84 CAL.APP.4TH 1163, 1170.) THE EVIDENCE DEMONSTRATES THAT THE PLAINTIFFS' PAY STUBS REFERRED TO MULTIPLE DIGNITY ENTITIES AND MOVANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF NAVA WAS NOT GENUINELY IGNORANT OF MOVANT'S IDENTITY WHEN SHE FILED SUIT. (COWAN DEC., EX. A; FULLER, 84 CAL.APP.4TH AT 1170 ["IT IS WHEN [PLAINTIFF] IS ACTUALLY IGNORANT OF A CERTAIN FACT, NOT WHEN [PLAINTIFF] MIGHT BY THE USE OF REASONABLE DILIGENCE HAVE DISCOVERED IT. WHETHER [PLAINTIFF'S] IGNORANCE IS FROM MISFORTUNE OR NEGLIGENCE, [PLAINTIFF] IS ALIKE IGNORANT, AND THIS IS ALL THE STATUTE REQUIRES."]; MCOWEN V. GROSSMAN (2007) 153 CAL.APP.4TH 937, 943-944 ["THE FACT THAT THE PLAINTIFF HAD THE MEANS TO OBTAIN KNOWLEDGE IS IRRELEVANT."].) THE COURT NOTES THAT MOVANT HAS BEEN LITIGATING THIS ACTION IN ITS OWN NAME SINCE IT ANSWERED THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT ON MARCH 16, 2018. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Of Amended Complaint On Dignity Health Medical Foundation; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Oct-27-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Barbara E. Cowan of the law offices of Workplace Advocates LLC (barbara@theworkplaceadvocates.com) specially appears as counsel for opposing party, plaintiff Katherine Abell

Attorney, Daniel McQueen of the law offices of SHEPPARDMULLIN (DMcQueen@sheppardmullin.com) appears as counsel for moving party, defendant Dignity Health, Inc.

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides, on record as reflected in the notes of the certified shorthand reporter.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH MEDICAL FOUNDATION'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Of Amended Complaint On Dignity Health Medical Foundation is DENIED in its entirety. The motion is denied as to plaintiffs Abell, Driemeyer, and Hall as movant concedes that it has generally appeared in their action and no longer seeks to quash service of the summons. (Reply, 2:1-6.) The motion is denied as to plaintiff Nava. "Code of Civil Procedure section 474 is to be liberally construed." (Fuller v. Tucker (2000) 84 Cal.App.4th 1163, 1170.) The evidence demonstrates that the plaintiffs' pay stubs referred to multiple Dignity entities and movant fails to show that plaintiff Nava was not genuinely ignorant of movant's identity when she filed suit. (Cowan Dec., Ex. A; Fuller, 84 Cal.App.4th at 1170 ["It is when [plaintiff] is actually ignorant of a certain fact, not when [plaintiff] might by the use of reasonable diligence have discovered it. Whether [plaintiff's] ignorance is from misfortune or negligence, [plaintiff] is alike ignorant, and this is all the statute requires."]; McOwen v. Grossman (2007) 153 Cal.App.4th 937, 943-944 ["The fact that the plaintiff had the means to obtain knowledge is irrelevant."].) The Court notes that movant has been litigating this action in its own name since it answered the original complaint on March 16, 2018. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com.

MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-19-576504 WITH CASE CGC-18-563862
Off Calendar Mar-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Barbara E. Figari, Esq. of THE FIGARI LAW FIRM, T:626.486.2620, for plaintiffs/ moving party, on Court Call;
David A. Alvarez, Esq. of SHEPPARD MULLIN RICHTER & HAMPTON LLP, T:213.455.7773, for defendant Dignity Health.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. After hearing, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFFS KATHERINE ABELL, NENA DRIEMEYER, CRYSTAL HALL's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-19-576504 WITH CASE CGC-18-563862 as follows;

Plaintiff's motion to consolidate cases CGC-18-563862 and CGC-19-576504 for all purposes is granted. The court designates case CGC-18-563862 as the lead case and all future filings shall be made under that case number.

When ruling on a motion to consolidate the court considers the common issues of law and fact, timeliness (trial date/discovery), complexity of the cases, and prejudice to the parties.

Here, the above factors weigh in favor of consolidation. Both actions involve common issues of law and fact, i.e., whether defendant has a pattern of paying female behavioral health clinicians less than their male counterparts. The motion is timely. While the Abell action has been pending longer and is more developed, it currently has no trial date. Much of the discovery that has been conducted in Abell is relevant to the Nava action. A consolidated action, which includes the PAGA claim, will not be unduly complex. The prejudice to defendant is minimal and judicial economy will be served by coordinated discovery and one trier of fact resolving all of the claims.

Counsel for plaintiff is to prepare and submit a proposed order that repeats verbatim the Court's ruling.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
